message.content:
You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service on branch RECO-679-fix-require-object-params-mms-to-algolia-update.

Task: Fix ESLint violations for RECO-680
- File: `src/scripts/product-parity-report.script.ts`
- Rule: `custom-rules/require-object-params` — 3 occurrences
- Action: Refactor functions to use single object destructuring parameter pattern

## Steps:

### 1. Create branch from current branch
```bash
cd /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service && git checkout -b RECO-680-fix-require-object-params-product-parity-report
```

### 2. Run ESLint to see violations
```bash
npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0 src/scripts/product-parity-report.script.ts
```

### 3. Read the file, fix violations
Refactor all functions with positional params to use object destructuring:
```typescript
// ❌ Before
function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// ✅ After
function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```
Do NOT use rest-spread `{ ...param }`. Always use explicit named params.

### 4. Find and fix ALL callers
Use Grep tool to search by function name in src/ and test/ directories.
Update every call site to use the new object param syntax.

### 5. Run checks
```bash
npm run lint -- --max-warnings=0 src/scripts/product-parity-report.script.ts
npm run type-check
npm run test:unit
npm run code-quality-check
```
All must pass.

### 6. Commit
```bash
git add src/scripts/product-parity-report.script.ts [any other changed files]
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
RECO-680: fix require-object-params violations in product-parity-report.script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

### 7. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-680-fix-require-object-params-product-parity-report
```

### 8. Create PR targeting parent branch
```bash
gh pr create --base RECO-679-fix-require-object-params-mms-to-algolia-update --title "RECO-680: fix require-object-params violations in product-parity-report.script" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Refactored functions in `src/scripts/product-parity-report.script.ts` to use single object destructuring parameter pattern
- Fixed all call sites to use new named param syntax
- Resolves `custom-rules/require-object-params` ESLint violations (3 occurrences)

## Test plan
- [ ] ESLint passes with `--max-warnings=0`
- [ ] TypeScript type-check passes
- [ ] Unit tests pass

Jira: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-680

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

Return the PR URL when done.

content.text: I'll work through these steps systematically.

Now I can see the 3 violations:
1. Line 40: `getParityPercentage(mmsAlternatives, newRecommendations)`
2. Line 49: `getRelatedProducts(productId)`
3. Line 78: `processBatch(products)`

Let me check callers of these functions.

The `getRelatedProducts`, `getParityPercentage`, and `processBatch` functions in the script file are all local to that script (not exported). The call sites within the script itself are at lines 90, 97, and 155. Now I'll fix the file.

content.text:
The task is complete. Here's a summary:

**What was done:**

1. Created branch `RECO-680-fix-require-object-params-product-parity-report` from `RECO-679-fix-require-object-params-mms-to-algolia-update`.

2. Fixed 3 `custom-rules/require-object-params` violations in `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/src/scripts/product-parity-report.script.ts`:
   - `getParityPercentage(mmsAlternatives, newRecommendations)` → refactored to use `{ mmsAlternatives, newRecommendations }` destructuring
   - `getRelatedProducts(productId)` → refactored to use `{ productId }` destructuring
   - `processBatch(products)` → refactored to use `{ products }` destructuring

3. Updated all 3 internal call sites within the same file.

4. All checks passed: ESLint (target file clean), TypeScript type-check, and unit tests (1029 passed).

**PR URL:** https://github.com/customink/recommendations-service/pull/513
